description: One who accompanies another; a fellow traveler on the path. 'Keloe' names who walks beside—not ahead or behind but alongside.
sound_symbolism: The word keeps with presence. 'Ke' holds with abiding company, companionship maintained. 'Lo' opens with shared path, journey together. Then 'e' eases into comfortable togetherness.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'keloe nua mia thalo.' (The companion walks with me.) Related to 'melu' (friend) but emphasizes shared journey. One who shares 'thole' (practice).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Kalyana-mitta—the spiritual friend who accompanies on the path.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Companion as bread-sharer—the original meaning, those who break bread together.
